root_text.an3.30:1.3: Avakujjapañño puggalo, ucchaṅgapañño puggalo, puthupañño puggalo.
root_text.an3.30:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo ārāmaṁ gantā hoti abhikkhaṇaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ santike dhammassavanāya.
root_text.an3.30:1.6: Tassa bhikkhū dhammaṁ desenti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsenti.
root_text.an3.30:1.7: So tasmiṁ āsane nisinno tassā kathāya neva ādiṁ manasi karoti, na majjhaṁ manasi karoti, na pariyosānaṁ manasi karoti;
root_text.an3.30:1.8: vuṭṭhitopi tamhā āsanā tassā kathāya neva ādiṁ manasi karoti, na majjhaṁ manasi karoti, na pariyosānaṁ manasi karoti.
root_text.an3.30:1.9: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, kumbho nikujjo tatra udakaṁ āsittaṁ vivaṭṭati, no saṇṭhāti;
root_text.an3.30:1.10: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo ārāmaṁ gantā hoti abhikkhaṇaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ santike dhammassavanāya.
root_text.an3.30:1.11: Tassa bhikkhū dhammaṁ desenti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsenti.
root_text.an3.30:1.12: So tasmiṁ āsane nisinno tassā kathāya neva ādiṁ manasi karoti, na majjhaṁ manasi karoti, na pariyosānaṁ manasi karoti;
root_text.an3.30:1.13: vuṭṭhitopi tamhā āsanā tassā kathāya neva ādiṁ manasi karoti, na majjhaṁ manasi karoti, na pariyosānaṁ manasi karoti.
root_text.an3.30:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo ārāmaṁ gantā hoti abhikkhaṇaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ santike dhammassavanāya.
root_text.an3.30:2.3: Tassa bhikkhū dhammaṁ desenti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsenti.
root_text.an3.30:2.4: So tasmiṁ āsane nisinno tassā kathāya ādimpi manasi karoti, majjhampi manasi karoti, pariyosānampi manasi karoti;
root_text.an3.30:2.5: vuṭṭhito ca kho tamhā āsanā tassā kathāya neva ādiṁ manasi karoti, na majjhaṁ manasi karoti, na pariyosānaṁ manasi karoti.
root_text.an3.30:2.6: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, purisassa ucchaṅge nānākhajjakāni ākiṇṇāni—
root_text.an3.30:2.8: So tamhā āsanā vuṭṭhahanto satisammosā pakireyya.
root_text.an3.30:2.9: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo ārāmaṁ gantā hoti abhikkhaṇaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ santike dhammassavanāya.
root_text.an3.30:2.10: Tassa bhikkhū dhammaṁ desenti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsenti.
root_text.an3.30:2.11: So tasmiṁ āsane nisinno tassā kathāya ādimpi manasi karoti, majjhampi manasi karoti, pariyosānampi manasi karoti;
root_text.an3.30:2.12: vuṭṭhito ca kho tamhā āsanā tassā kathāya neva ādiṁ manasi karoti, na majjhaṁ manasi karoti, na pariyosānaṁ manasi karoti.
root_text.an3.30:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo ārāmaṁ gantā hoti abhikkhaṇaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ santike dhammassavanāya.
root_text.an3.30:3.3: Tassa bhikkhū dhammaṁ desenti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsenti.
root_text.an3.30:3.4: So tasmiṁ āsane nisinno tassā kathāya ādimpi manasi karoti, majjhampi manasi karoti, pariyosānampi manasi karoti;
root_text.an3.30:3.5: vuṭṭhitopi tamhā āsanā tassā kathāya ādimpi manasi karoti, majjhampi manasi karoti, pariyosānampi manasi karoti.
root_text.an3.30:3.6: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, kumbho ukkujjo tatra udakaṁ āsittaṁ saṇṭhāti no vivaṭṭati;
root_text.an3.30:3.7: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo ārāmaṁ gantā hoti abhikkhaṇaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ santike dhammassavanāya.
root_text.an3.30:3.8: Tassa bhikkhū dhammaṁ desenti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsenti.
root_text.an3.30:3.9: So tasmiṁ āsane nisinno tassā kathāya ādimpi manasi karoti, majjhampi manasi karoti, pariyosānampi manasi karoti;
root_text.an3.30:3.10: vuṭṭhitopi tamhā āsanā tassā kathāya ādimpi manasi karoti, majjhampi manasi karoti, pariyosānampi manasi karoti.
root_text.an3.30:3.12: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasminti.

translation_text.an3.30:1.1: “These three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.30:1.3: One with upside-down wisdom, one with wisdom on their lap, and one with widespread wisdom.
translation_text.an3.30:1.4: And who is the individual with upside-down wisdom?
translation_text.an3.30:1.5: It’s an individual who often goes to the monastery to hear the teaching in the presence of the mendicants.
translation_text.an3.30:1.6: The mendicants teach them Dhamma that’s good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And they reveal a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.an3.30:1.7: But even while sitting there, that person doesn’t apply the mind to the beginning, middle, or end of the discussion.
translation_text.an3.30:1.8: And when they get up from their seat, they don’t apply the mind to the beginning, middle, or end of the discussion.
translation_text.an3.30:1.9: It’s like when a pot full of water is tipped over, so the water drains out and doesn’t stay.
translation_text.an3.30:1.10: In the same way, an individual often goes to the monastery to hear the teaching in the presence of the mendicants.
translation_text.an3.30:1.11: The mendicants teach them Dhamma that’s good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And they reveal a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.an3.30:1.12: But even while sitting there, that person doesn’t apply the mind to the discussion in the beginning, middle, or end.
translation_text.an3.30:1.13: And when they get up from their seat, they don’t apply the mind to the beginning, middle, or end of the discussion.
translation_text.an3.30:1.14: This is called an individual with upside-down wisdom.
translation_text.an3.30:2.1: And who is the individual with wisdom on their lap?
translation_text.an3.30:2.2: It’s an individual who often goes to the monastery to hear the teaching in the presence of the mendicants.
translation_text.an3.30:2.3: The mendicants teach them Dhamma that’s good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And they reveal a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.an3.30:2.4: While sitting there, that person applies the mind to the discussion in the beginning, middle, and end.
translation_text.an3.30:2.5: But when they get up from their seat, they don’t apply the mind to the beginning, middle, or end of the discussion.
translation_text.an3.30:2.6: It’s like a person who has different kinds of food crammed on their lap—
translation_text.an3.30:2.7: such as sesame, rice, sweets, or jujube—
translation_text.an3.30:2.8: so that if they get up from the seat without mindfulness, everything gets scattered.
translation_text.an3.30:2.9: In the same way, an individual often goes to the monastery to hear the teaching in the presence of the mendicants.
translation_text.an3.30:2.10: The mendicants teach them Dhamma that’s good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And they reveal a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.an3.30:2.11: While sitting there, that person applies the mind to the discussion in the beginning, middle, and end.
translation_text.an3.30:2.12: But when they get up from their seat, they don’t apply the mind to the beginning, middle, or end of the discussion.
translation_text.an3.30:2.13: This is called an individual with wisdom on their lap.
translation_text.an3.30:3.1: And who is the individual with widespread wisdom?
translation_text.an3.30:3.2: It’s an individual who often goes to the monastery to hear the teaching in the presence of the mendicants.
translation_text.an3.30:3.3: The mendicants teach them Dhamma that’s good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And they reveal a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.an3.30:3.4: While sitting there, they apply the mind to the discussion in the beginning, middle, and end.
translation_text.an3.30:3.5: And when they get up from their seat, they continue to apply the mind to the beginning, middle, or end of the discussion.
translation_text.an3.30:3.6: It’s like when a pot full of water is set straight, so the water stays and doesn’t drain out.
translation_text.an3.30:3.7: In the same way, an individual often goes to the monastery to hear the teaching in the presence of the mendicants.
translation_text.an3.30:3.8: The mendicants teach them Dhamma that’s good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And they reveal a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.an3.30:3.9: While sitting there, they apply the mind to the discussion in the beginning, middle, and end.
translation_text.an3.30:3.10: And when they get up from their seat, they continue to apply the mind to the beginning, middle, or end of the discussion.
translation_text.an3.30:3.11: This is called an individual with widespread wisdom.
translation_text.an3.30:3.12: These are the three individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an3.30:4.2: is a simpleton and cannot see,
translation_text.an3.30:6.1: the individual with wisdom on their lap
translation_text.an3.30:6.2: is better than that, it’s said;
translation_text.an3.30:7.1: such a person can only learn
translation_text.an3.30:7.4: but they’ve only grasped the phrasing,
translation_text.an3.30:7.5: for when they get up their understanding fails,
translation_text.an3.30:7.6: and what they’ve learned is lost.
translation_text.an3.30:8.2: is better than that, it’s said;
translation_text.an3.30:9.4: and when they’ve grasped the phrasing,
translation_text.an3.30:10.1: they remember it with the best of intentions.
translation_text.an3.30:10.3: practicing in line with the teaching,
translation_text.an3.30:10.4: would make an end of suffering.”
